verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: I confess, I immediately set to work to reply to the insinuations directed against me, and tried with all my might to prove that I was no heretic, and I sent these books of my Apology to those whom your book had pained, so that your poison might be followed by my antidote.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: In reply to this, you sent me your former books, and now send me this last letter, full of injurious language and accusations.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: My good friend, what do you expect me to do?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: That would be to acknowledge myself guilty.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: But you hold your sword over my head, and threaten me with an indictment, no longer before the church but before the law-courts.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What have I done that deserves punishment?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Is it that I have shewn myself not to be a heretic? or that I could not esteem myself worthy of your praises? or that I laid bare in plain words the tricks and perjuries of the heretics?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What is all this to you who boast yourself a true man and a catholic, and who shew more zeal in attacking me than in defending yourself?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: Must I be thought to be attacking you because I defend myself? or is it impossible that you should be orthodox unless you prove me to be a heretic?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: What help can it give you to be connected with me? and what is the meaning of your action?

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: You are accused by one set of people and you answer only by attacking another.

verses.book: Jerome's Apology for Himself Against the Books of Rufinus.
verses.text: You find an attack made on you by one man, and you turn your back upon him and attack another who was for leaving you alone.
